---
pagetitle: "Survival Analysis Mini-Course"
description: "A build-along course on survival modelling for 3D medical imaging."
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hx-hero hx-reveal">
  <span class="hx-kicker">Mini Course</span>
  <h1>Survival Analysis</h1>
  <p class="hx-subtitle">A build-along course taking time-to-event modelling from first principles to a working 3D-imaging pipeline.</p>
  <div class="hx-cta">
    <a class="hx-btn hx-btn-solid" href="01_background/1.1_what_is_survival_analysis.html">Start the course →</a>
    <a class="hx-btn hx-btn-ghost" href="https://github.com/Safwat08/survival_minicourse">GitHub repository</a>
  </div>
</div>

<p class="hx-label">Course outline</p>

<table class="hx-outline">
  <tr>
    <td class="hx-num">01</td>
    <td class="hx-what"><strong>Background</strong><span>What survival analysis is — censoring, events, competing risks, and what clinicians want from a model.</span></td>
    <td class="hx-go"><a href="01_background/1.1_what_is_survival_analysis.html">Jump &rarr;</a></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="hx-num">02</td>
    <td class="hx-what"><strong>Loss Functions</strong><span>Cox partial likelihood, the discrete-time negative log-likelihood, DeepHit, and how they compare.</span></td>
    <td class="hx-go"><a href="02_loss_functions/2.1_cox_ph_loss.html">Jump &rarr;</a></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="hx-num">03</td>
    <td class="hx-what"><strong>Metrics</strong><span>C-index, Brier score, Kaplan-Meier curves, and time-dependent AUC.</span></td>
    <td class="hx-go"><a href="03_metrics/3.1_c_index.html">Jump &rarr;</a></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="hx-num">04</td>
    <td class="hx-what"><strong>Packages</strong><span>pycox, lifelines, torchsurv, scikit-survival, auton-survival — routed by how much of the training loop you own.</span></td>
    <td class="hx-go"><a href="04_packages/4.1_pycox.html">Jump &rarr;</a></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="hx-num">05</td>
    <td class="hx-what"><strong>Recipes</strong><span>End-to-end progression-free-survival risk stratification.</span></td>
    <td class="hx-go"><a href="06_recipes/6.1_pfs_stratification.html">Jump &rarr;</a></td>
  </tr>
</table>
```

## How to read it

Start at the top and follow the cross-links. Notation is introduced once, where
the concept lives, and referenced everywhere else — so if a symbol is unfamiliar,
the link beside it points to its definition.

Figures throughout are generated deterministically from
[`scripts/make_figures.py`](scripts/make_figures.py); the synthetic examples are
chosen for clarity, and the runnable model code targets the built-in GBSG2
dataset.
